basics.name: Colin Aten
basics.label: Computer Engineering Student
basics.aboutme: I'm a Computer Engineering student at the University of Delaware with a passion creating function solutions using software. Whether I'm developing signal-based monitoring systems, creating web applications, or tinkering with my own home server setups, I love tackling complex problems from the ground up. Beyond the screen and the breadboard, I thrive on staying active and creative. You'll often find me on the water sailing, hitting the slopes for some skiing, or jumping into a fast-paced game of pickleball. I'm also deeply passionate about music—whether I'm laying down a groove on the bass or singing in a choir. I believe the best engineering comes from a well-rounded perspective, and I bring that same collaborative energy and discipline to every project I take on.
basics.summary: Computer Engineering student at the University of Delaware with hands-on experience in embedded systems, full-stack development, and control systems integration. Demonstrated initiative through end-to-end personal projects involving real-time data processing, cloud infrastructure, and signal-based sensing. Eager to apply analytical and engineering fundamentals to mission-level problems.

highlights: Honors College
Computer Science Minor
Cybersecurity Minor
German for Engineers Program
IEEE Honor Society Tau Beta Pi

work.name: Multi-Dimensional Integration
highlights: Designed and deployed a FastAPI-based Python tool to automate JSON configuration editing for Ignition SCADA projects, reducing manual workflow time and eliminating a class of human error in config management
Built a custom Python library for programmatic manipulation of Ignition JSON data structures, enabling repeatable and testable configuration workflows across multiple projects
Developed interactive Python and Java interfaces within the Ignition platform to improve operator visibility and control over live industrial processes
Extended legacy control system projects by interpreting engineering drawings and technical documentation, validating outputs against original design specifications
Collaborated within a multidisciplinary engineering team to scope, implement, and deliver features against project management milestones

work.name: Johns Hopkins University, Department of Epidemiology
highlights: Developed JavaScript tooling for clinical trial data entry forms, improving data validation and reducing entry errors across active studies
Built a PHP program to convert and migrate a Microsoft Access database to a web-compatible structure, preserving referential integrity across thousands of records
Maintained and improved client-server architecture for web-based data collection forms used in ongoing epidemiological research
Worked iteratively with researchers and staff to incorporate user feedback into system improvements under real-world data integrity constraints

work.name: University of Delaware, Dept. of Electrical & Computer Engineering
work.position: Lab Assistant – Analog Circuits I & Digital Systems
highlights: Guided students hands-on through analog circuit construction, measurement, and debugging in weekly lab sections
Led a lab section on digital systems, mentoring students through VHDL design projects from specification to synthesis and simulation
Diagnosed and explained circuit and code failures, building student intuition for both hardware and HDL debugging

work.name: Mid Atlantic Community Church
highlights: Configured and operated ProPresenter software for live in-house displays and simultaneous online streaming
Shot and edited marketing and informational videos using DSLR cameras and Adobe Premiere Pro
Installed, configured, and maintained an indoor stage lighting system and various AV electronics

projects.name: CradleWave Baby Monitor
projects.description: End-to-end IoT sensing system using 60 GHz millimeter-wave radar for non-contact infant monitoring
highlights: Designed a sensing pipeline using a 60 GHz FMCW radar module to detect infant presence, motion, and breathing rate without physical contact

Implemented signal processing on raw radar returns to extract breathing rate and movement metrics; evaluated detection performance against ground-truth observations across varying distance and orientation scenarios
Built a Python FastAPI backend to ingest real-time radar data, apply processing algorithms, and stream results to cloud storage via Google Firebase
Developed a Vue.js frontend and mobile-accessible web app for live monitoring dashboards and historical data review
Validated system robustness across multiple environmental conditions; characterized false-alarm rate and detection latency as key performance indicators

projects.name: Ignition JSON Automation Tool
projects.description: Python tooling for automating configuration workflows in Ignition SCADA environments
highlights: Built a FastAPI application exposing a REST interface for programmatic JSON editing of Ignition project configurations
Developed a reusable internal Python library abstracting common Ignition JSON data manipulation patterns
Reduced configuration turnaround time and introduced testability into previously manual processes

projects.name: RemAIk – Full-Stack Browser Game
projects.description: Multiplayer browser-based game with AI-generated content and secure user authentication
highlights: Built a full-stack multiplayer game using Vue.js frontend and Google Firebase for real-time database and authentication
Designed a secure user-focused data model with role-based access controls and authenticated session management
Integrated an AI content generation pipeline to dynamically produce game prompts and assets

projects.name: Arduino Shield Cloud Timer
projects.description: Custom Arduino shield with cloud-connected dashboard for timer monitoring and control
highlights: Designed a custom PCB Arduino shield integrating multiple external peripherals including display, input, and wireless modules
Wrote firmware to control and display a configurable timer with cloud telemetry via MQTT
Built a web dashboard to visualize live and historical timer data sent from the device to the cloud

volunteer.summary: Lead a campus ministry community at the University of Delaware
highlights: Coordinated event planning with university administration for events of 50+ students
Grew a collaborative campus community through intentional relationship building and programming

awards.title: Eagle Scout
awards.summary: Highest rank in Scouting. Served as Senior Patrol Leader, Philmont Crew Leader, and held multiple other leadership positions over 7 years. Planned annual programs and weekly meetings, leading groups of up to 70 scouts across campouts, high-adventure treks, and service projects.

awards.title: Tau Beta Pi – IEEE Honor Society
awards.summary: Member of the engineering honor society recognizing academic excellence and commitment to the profession.
